title: 10 precautions to take when using Tabebuia rosea (Pau D’Arco) for medicinal purposes
intro: Tabebuia rosea, also known as Pau d'Arco, is a tree with medicinal properties, but it should be used with caution. It's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedy. Overdosing or prolonged use may lead to adverse effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should avoid its use due to lack of sufficient research on its safety. Additionally, it can interact with certain medications, so it's crucial to inform your doctor of any herbal supplements you are taking. Lastly, always ensure you are using high-quality, pure Tabebuia rosea products from reputable sources to minimize the risk of contamination or adulteration.
